Title: The bZIP transcription factor RISBZ1 balances grain filling and ER stress response in rice grains

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: grain filling and dry matter accumulation; determines grain quality
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; RISBZ1/bZIP58; ortholog of maize O2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: storage reserve biosynthesis and unfolded protein response during grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of storage protein and starch genes; transcriptional repression of UPR output indirectly by protein–protein inhibition of bZIP50/bZIP60 DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GluA2; AGPL3; SSP genes; starch biosynthesis-related genes; indirectly BiP1; PDIL1-1; PDIL1-4; PDIL2-1; PDIL2-3; NEF; CRT via bZIP60/bZIP50 antagonism
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SSP and starch biosynthesis genes; suppresses expression of multiple UPR genes under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ER stress; high temperature; DTT treatment; endosperm developmental program
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: RISBZ1 expression is upregulated in bzip60, suggesting stress-responsive/compensatory feedback with UPR branch
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms nuclear complexes with bZIP50 and bZIP60 under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central balancing regulator linking grain filling with ER stress adaptation; acts as a brake on UPR while promoting dry matter accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; complementation; CRISPR knockout; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; subcellular localization; yeast two-hybrid; pull-down; luciferase complementation; BiFC; dual-LUC; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; double-mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes starch and storage protein accumulation and grain filling; loss causes floury/chalky endosperm, reduced starch/protein, severe ER stress and UPR activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLO27 was cloned as RISBZ1/bZIP58, and mutation caused reduced starch, amylose, and protein accumulation with floury endosperm. 2. RISBZ1 is a nucleus-localized endosperm-specific bZIP that activates storage protein and starch biosynthesis genes. 3. RISBZ1 physically interacts with bZIP50 and bZIP60 during ER stress and suppresses their DNA-binding/transcriptional activation of UPR genes. 4. Reciprocal inhibition between RISBZ1 and bZIP50/60 compromises storage reserve gene activation, explaining the trade-off between grain filling and ER-stress adaptation. 5. Double mutants with bzip50 or bzip60 enhance chalkiness, ER stress markers, and grain-filling defects, and high temperature aggravates these phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ER stress induced by DTT; high temperature during grain filling (35°C light/28°C dark) aggravates mutant phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native 2-kb RISBZ1 promoter for complementation; CaMV35S and Ubiquitin promoters attempted for overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: UPR suppression by RISBZ1 is coupled to reduced ability to sustain stress adaptation if absent; interaction with bZIP50/60 represses storage reserve gene expression under stress, reducing dry matter accumulation; mutant lines show decreased grain weight and chalkiness
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: optimize RISBZ1 expression/haplotype together with bZIP50 and bZIP60 to improve grain quality and heat/ER-stress resilience; avoid over- or under-expression that disrupts ER homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RISBZ1-Hap2 and RISBZ1-Hap6 were positively selected in japonica and indica, respectively, suggesting contribution to regional adaptation during domestication

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports proper grain filling and grain quality through ER proteostasis
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum under non-stress; nucleus after ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing endosperm; induced in flo27 and under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: UPR signaling affecting storage reserve accumulation indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of specific UPR genes; represses RISBZ1-mediated activation of storage reserve genes through protein interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates selected UPR genes and dampens RISBZ1 activation of GluA2 and AGPL3 when interacting with RISBZ1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ER stress; DTT treatment; intrinsic stress in flo27
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: bZIP50 expression is induced in bzip60, suggesting compensatory feedback between UPR branches
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms nuclear complex with RISBZ1 under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RISBZ1; bZIP60 (compensatory branch relationship inferred, not direct interaction shown)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: contributes to UPR buffering in endosperm; effect is weaker than bZIP60 but still impacts chalkiness and grain filling under stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; subcellular localization; yeast two-hybrid; pull-down; luciferase complementation; BiFC; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; double-mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss causes mild chalkiness and altered UPR gene expression; in risbz1 background strongly exacerbates floury phenotype, reduced grain filling, and ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP50 is an ER-localized membrane-associated bZIP that translocates to the nucleus after DTT-induced ER stress. 2. It directly binds and activates promoters of several UPR genes including PDIL1-4 and NEF. 3. bzip50 knockout causes mild chalkiness and mixed expression changes of UPR genes, indicating a contributory but partly compensated role. 4. bZIP50 interacts with RISBZ1, and this mutual interaction suppresses RISBZ1-driven storage reserve gene activation. 5. Combined loss with RISBZ1 causes stronger ER stress, chalkiness, and reduced grain weight, especially under high temperature.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DTT-induced ER stress; high temperature during grain filling intensifies phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: double CaMV35S for localization; reporter promoters PDIL1-4 and NEF in LUC assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: misregulation perturbs ER homeostasis and can impair grain quality; double mutants are especially heat sensitive
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: tune expression jointly with RISBZ1 and bZIP60 rather than single-gene extremes to improve low-chalkiness grain under heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype appears environment-sensitive and milder than previously reported under warmer locations

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports ER proteostasis required for normal grain filling and grain quality
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum under non-stress; nucleus after ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing endosperm; induced in flo27 and under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: UPR signaling affecting endosperm protein folding environment and storage reserve accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation of UPR genes; represses RISBZ1-mediated activation of storage reserve genes via protein interaction
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CRT; PDIL1-4; PDIL2-1; PDIL2-3; ERDJ3; BiP1; NEF; PDIL1-1 (activated in reporter and promoter occupancy assays)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates multiple UPR downstream genes and antagonizes RISBZ1 activation of GluA2 and AGPL3
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ER stress; DTT treatment; intrinsic ER stress in flo27; high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: loss of bZIP60 induces RISBZ1 and bZIP50 expression, suggesting compensatory feedback
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms nuclear complex with RISBZ1 under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major UPR branch regulator in rice endosperm; stronger determinant than bZIP50 for maintaining low chalkiness and proper reserve deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; subcellular localization; yeast two-hybrid; pull-down; luciferase complementation; BiFC; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; double-mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss causes floury endosperm similar to flo27, strong elevation of UPR markers, and enhanced defects when combined with risbz1; high temperature aggravates defects
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP60 is an ER stress transducer that relocates from the ER to the nucleus after DTT treatment and in flo27 endosperm. 2. It directly binds promoters of multiple UPR genes and activates their transcription. 3. bzip60 mutants show a floury endosperm phenotype and strong accumulation of BiP1 and PDIL1-1, indicating disrupted ER homeostasis. 4. RISBZ1 interacts with bZIP60 and suppresses its DNA binding to UPR gene promoters, while bZIP60 suppresses RISBZ1 activation of storage reserve genes. 5. ChIP-qPCR showed stronger enrichment of bZIP60 on UPR gene promoters in flo27, linking RISBZ1 loss to elevated UPR output.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DTT-induced ER stress; high temperature during grain filling exacerbates chalkiness and grain shrinkage
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: double CaMV35S for localization; BiP1, PDIL1-1, PDIL1-4, and NEF promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: both loss and misregulation disrupt ER homeostasis and grain quality; stress-sensitive background under high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: key target to fine-tune UPR capacity for low-chalkiness, heat-resilient grain, preferably combined with optimized RISBZ1 haplotypes/expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude bZIP60 plays a more prominent role than bZIP50 in rice UPR during grain filling
